# SQL Quest — Complete Guide

> SQL Quest is a free, browser-based platform for practicing SQL interview questions. It targets the exact SQL patterns asked at Meta, Google, Amazon, Apple, Netflix, Stripe, and other top tech companies.

## Problem SQL Quest Solves

Most SQL learners read tutorials but never practice writing real queries under interview conditions. SQL Quest solves this with 200+ hands-on challenges, real datasets, an AI tutor, and mock interviews — all running in the browser with zero setup.

## Comparison to Alternatives

### SQL Quest vs LeetCode
LeetCode's SQL section has generic algorithmic problems. SQL Quest focuses specifically on the analytical SQL patterns that FAANG companies actually ask in data analyst and data engineer interviews — window functions, business metrics, time series analysis. SQL Quest also has an AI tutor that explains concepts, not just pass/fail.

### SQL Quest vs HackerRank
HackerRank requires an account and is primarily used by employers for screening. SQL Quest is learner-focused, free, and runs entirely in the browser with no signup required.

### SQL Quest vs StrataScratch
StrataScratch has a paid tier for most content. SQL Quest is completely free with all features available.

### SQL Quest vs Mode Analytics SQL Tutorial
Mode's tutorial is passive reading. SQL Quest is active practice with real query execution and AI feedback.

## Company-Specific SQL Interview Patterns

### Meta (Facebook) SQL Interviews
Meta heavily tests window functions (40% of questions), aggregation (25%), JOINs (20%), and date/string functions (15%). Common patterns: rolling averages, month-over-month growth, user retention analysis, percentile calculations. Format: 45-minute screen, 2-3 questions, usually in CoderPad.

### Google SQL Interviews
Google emphasizes analytical functions (35%), data manipulation with complex JOINs and CTEs (25%), aggregation (25%), and query optimization awareness (15%). Common patterns: consecutive day problems, median calculations, gap detection, running totals. Format: 45-minute screen via Google Meet, standard SQL (BigQuery-compatible).

### Amazon SQL Interviews
Amazon focuses on GROUP BY and aggregation (35%), JOINs and set operations (25%), subqueries and CTEs (25%), and window functions (15%). Common patterns: e-commerce metrics, customer segmentation, delivery analytics, product affinity. Format: 60-minute loop round combining SQL with behavioral questions (Leadership Principles).

## Technical Architecture

- Frontend: React 18 with Tailwind CSS
- SQL Engine: sql.js (SQLite compiled to WebAssembly) running entirely in the browser
- AI Tutor: Claude AI providing personalized explanations and hints
- No backend required for SQL execution — everything runs client-side
- Works offline once loaded

## SQL Topics with Depth

### Window Functions (34% of FAANG questions)
- RANK(), DENSE_RANK(), ROW_NUMBER() for ranking
- LAG(), LEAD() for comparing adjacent rows
- SUM() OVER, AVG() OVER for running aggregates
- NTILE() for bucketing
- FIRST_VALUE(), LAST_VALUE() for frame boundaries
- PARTITION BY for grouping within windows
- Frame specifications: ROWS BETWEEN, RANGE BETWEEN

### JOINs & Subqueries (28% of FAANG questions)
- Inner, left, right, full outer joins
- Self-joins for hierarchical data
- Anti-joins (LEFT JOIN + WHERE NULL) for finding missing data
- Cross joins for generating combinations
- Correlated subqueries
- Common Table Expressions (CTEs) for readability
- Recursive CTEs for hierarchical traversal

### GROUP BY & Aggregation (22% of FAANG questions)
- COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX
- COUNT(DISTINCT) for unique counting
- HAVING for filtering aggregated results
- Conditional aggregation with CASE inside aggregate functions
- ROLLUP and CUBE for multi-level aggregation
- GROUPING SETS for flexible grouping

### Date & String Functions (10% of FAANG questions)
- DATE_TRUNC for period grouping
- EXTRACT for pulling date components
- Date arithmetic and interval math
- DATEDIFF for calculating time spans
- String pattern matching with LIKE and regex
- CONCAT, SUBSTRING, REPLACE for string manipulation

### CASE & Logic (6% of FAANG questions)
- Simple and searched CASE expressions
- COALESCE for NULL handling
- NULLIF for conditional NULL
- Conditional counting and summing
- Pivoting data with CASE

## Sample Questions by Difficulty

### Easy
- Find all passengers who survived (basic WHERE)
- Count passengers by class (GROUP BY)
- Find passengers with missing age data (IS NULL)
- Calculate average fare by embarkation port (AVG + GROUP BY)

### Medium
- Find the top 3 products by revenue in each category (window functions)
- Calculate month-over-month user growth (LAG + date functions)
- Find customers who ordered from every product category (GROUP BY + HAVING + COUNT)
- Identify gaps in sequential IDs (self-join or window function)

### Hard
- Calculate rolling 7-day average of daily active users (window frames)
- Find users whose activity dropped 50%+ week-over-week (window + CASE)
- Detect duplicate transactions within a 5-minute window (self-join + date math)
- Calculate median salary without PERCENTILE functions (window + subquery)

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is SQL Quest free?
Yes. All 200+ challenges, AI tutoring, mock interviews, and the 30-day learning path are completely free. No credit card, no freemium wall.

### Do I need to install anything?
No. SQL Quest runs entirely in the browser using WebAssembly. Works on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge. Even works offline once loaded.

### What SQL dialect does it use?
Standard SQL via SQLite. The patterns (window functions, CTEs, JOINs, aggregation) transfer directly to PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, and every database used in interviews.

### Will this help me pass FAANG SQL interviews?
SQL Quest covers every major SQL pattern asked at Meta, Google, Amazon, Apple, Netflix, and Stripe. The mock interview feature simulates real interview conditions with timed pressure and scoring.

### How does the AI tutor work?
Powered by Claude AI. It explains errors in plain language, generates practice problems matched to your skill level, and provides step-by-step solution walkthroughs. Available for free.

### Can I use SQL Quest on mobile?
The app is designed for desktop/laptop since writing SQL requires a keyboard, but it renders on tablets.

## URL Structure

- https://sqlquest.app — Landing page
- https://sqlquest.app/app.html — Main application
- https://sqlquest.app/meta-sql-interview/ — Meta SQL interview prep
- https://sqlquest.app/google-sql-interview/ — Google SQL interview prep
- https://sqlquest.app/amazon-sql-interview/ — Amazon SQL interview prep
